task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris main menu or application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the Tic-Tac-Toe activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tic-Tac-Toe game board (a 3x3 grid) is visible within the GCompris interface.

task_completion.description: Ensure the game is set to Level 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level indicator in the GCompris interface shows Level 1 (typically represented by a single highlighted star or the number 1).

task_completion.description: Win the game of Tic-Tac-Toe against the computer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The game board shows three of the player's marks in a row (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) and a victory screen, animation, or indicator is displayed.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the victory screen to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command, save dialog, or file manager shows a screenshot being saved to or existing at '/home/ga/tic_tac_toe_victory.png'.

integrity.description: The agent actually played the game rather than just downloading or displaying a static image of a win
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows progressive moves being made on the board over time.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris application
integrity.visual_evidence: The game interface matches GCompris's distinct visual style, rather than a web browser or a different Tic-Tac-Toe program.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot corresponds to the actual gameplay session
integrity.visual_evidence: The final saved screenshot matches the exact game state and victory screen achieved during the GUI interaction steps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is exactly '/home/ga/tic_tac_toe_victory.png'. The activity is 'Tic-Tac-Toe' (or 'Tic Tac Toe'), which is located in the 'Board games' category of GCompris. A minimum of 5 moves is required to achieve a win.
